mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive returns with a statistical significance level of p less than 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days specifically during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative forward returns in greater than 70 percent of expansion-phase observations, or the signal loses predictive power when GDP growth exceeds 3.0 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly boost aggregate wage income and consumer purchasing power, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 5-day forward returns, yielding a Rank IC below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 230, elevated inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces monetary tightening expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 230 consistently precede positive forward returns, or inflation shocks show no statistically significant negative correlation with equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly real personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based economic momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Divergence between GDP and consumption growth eliminates predictive accuracy, or joint growth signals yield negative returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index signal manufacturing contraction and reduced capacity utilization, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days exclusively during late-cycle economic phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines predict positive returns in greater than 55 percent of late-cycle observations, or the mechanism shows no directional bias when tested against historical late-cycle periods.
